source_title: Two-year effects of semaglutide in adults with overweight or obesity: the STEP 5 trial
research_question: Do GLP-1 receptor agonists reduce body weight in adults with overweight or obesity?
claim_text: In the STEP 5 trial, once-weekly semaglutide 2.4 mg provided evidence of greater body-weight reduction than placebo at week 104 in adults with overweight or obesity without diabetes.
population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
intervention: Once-weekly subcutaneous semaglutide 2.4 mg plus behavioral intervention.
outcome: Percentage change in body weight from baseline to week 104.
result_summary: The paper reports an estimated mean body-weight change from baseline to week 104 of -15.2% with semaglutide versus -2.6% with placebo, with an estimated treatment difference of -12.6 percentage points.

source_span.locator_note: The same result also appears in the abstract on page 1 and Table 2 on page 5 of the local PDF.

short_source_excerpt: body weight from baseline to week 104 was -15.2% ... with semaglutide and -2.6% ... with placebo
limitations: Manual extraction from one paper only.
This record represents one trial result, not a corpus-level conclusion.
Participants were adults without diabetes and trial eligibility criteria limit generalizability.
Both groups received behavioral intervention, so the comparator is not no treatment.
The record does not yet include adverse-event details or discontinuation context.
uncertainty_notes: This evidence bears on the research question, but it does not prove the answer by itself. Later evidence records should preserve trial design, estimand, discontinuation handling, and population constraints.
confidence_note: High confidence that the extracted result is relevant to the research question and source-linked; not a confidence score for the scientific conclusion.

provenance.review_notes: Created as the first manual Evidence Layer prototype. No automated extraction was used.
